sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check for G objects
seg = current_frame.segmentation
g_objects = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
print(f"G objects: {len(g_objects)}")
for g in g_objects[:10]:
    print(f"  pixels={g['pixels']}, boundary={g['boundary']}")

# Check all colors
from collections import Counter
all_colors = Counter(n['color'] for n in seg['nodes'])
print(f"\nAll colors: {dict(all_colors)}")

# Try clicking G corners of first N block
# G corners at (25, 14), (25, 16), (29, 14), (29, 16)
result = action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 25, 'col': 14},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 25, 'col': 16},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 29, 'col': 14},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 29, 'col': 16}
])
print(f"\nResult: {result}")
print(f"Game over: {result.get('game_over', False)}")

# Check state
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
g_objects2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
print(f"G objects after: {len(g_objects2)}")
n_blocks2 = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"N blocks after: {len(n_blocks2)}")

# Check ascii
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nN block area after:")
for r in range(24, 37):
    print(f"  Row {r}: {ascii_lines[r][12:30]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
